tape: Weekend US-Iran missile exchange still overhangs the tape, but Monday session shows de-escalation signs (tankers resuming Hormuz transit, oil off its highs) even as S&P -0.4% / Nasdaq -1% on chip-stock weakness; VIX actually down 5% to 15.03, no panic, CPI print due Tuesday.

alerts.headline: Reverse-merger news: Boundless Bio (radar top-4 candidate, score 5.11) is merging with Serapha Bio in an all-stock deal, pivoting away from its own oncology pipeline to Serapha's base-editing AATD therapy; ~75% workforce cut and its own early cancer trials halted (one-time charges $3-5M in Q3). Stock reportedly +61.7% premarket. This is a distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength - high dilution/lock-up/structure uncertainty, avoid chasing.

Second consecutive snapshot where generic search surfaces a 'Mission Produce completed acquisition of Calavo Growers (May 2026)' claim alongside a stale/mismatched quote (~$11.50, -3.8%). Broker shows AVO flat ~$13.33. No primary-source confirmation of any acquisition found - treat as a recurring search-engine mixup with CVGW, not a real catalyst.

Caris Life Sciences (CAI1): Caris Detect multi-cancer blood test launch and NYSE Texas dual listing already known; Evercore ISI cut PT to $25 from $30 (still Outperform, still above current price). Broker shows CAI flat ~$16.99-17.15; no fresh negative catalyst for the -4.9% quote seen in search.
No new news; CEO Ross Bhappu's $968K buy (7/7) and the ~$1.9B Vacuumschmelze acquisition remain the latest known catalysts, both already priced in.
+14.4% move continues, unchanged from prior snapshot - still riding the 6/29 BLA filing, Wainwright Buy initiation, and 7/8 director buy ($167K). No new news today.
+4.9% move continues, unchanged from prior snapshot; still no new fundamental driver since the late-June EVO756 trial miss/downgrades.

Ticker ambiguity persists: generic search keeps surfacing Netskope (mid-cap, $5B) vs NetSol Technologies (NTWK, small-cap). No confirmed company-specific news found today for the held NTSK_US_EQ position.

Hormuz de-escalation (tankers resuming transit, oil off highs) is a mild fundamental headwind for offshore drillers, but the $1B Equinor Cat-D rig backlog remains the core support. No new negative news.
No fresh news; late-June PT hikes (Leerink/Guggenheim/BTIG) and the 6/24 $100M offering remain the latest known catalysts.
No fresh news since 5/18 board/management insider buying; DLA refrigerant contract extension already known.

movers.note: Reported +61.7% premarket on Serapha Bio reverse-merger announcement + own pipeline halt/75% workforce cut - distressed-merger pop, not fundamental strength.
+14.4%, still riding BLA/coverage/insider-buy momentum, no new news today.
